title: Images to PDF Converter
description: Combine multiple images into a PDF. Reorder pages, choose A4/Letter/original size, set margins — all in your browser.

buttons.generate: {count, plural, one {Generate PDF ({count} image)} other {Generate PDF ({count} images)}}

dropzone.title: Drop images here or click to select
dropzone.hint: PNG, JPG, WebP, BMP and more

imageList.title: {count, plural, one {{count} image added} other {{count} images added}}

howToUse.label: Add images
howToUse.description: Drag and drop images onto the drop zone, or click to select files from your device.

howToUse.label: Reorder pages
howToUse.description: Drag the rows in the image list to change the page order in the final PDF.

howToUse.label: Configure settings
howToUse.description: Choose the page size (A4, Letter, or original image dimensions) and set the margin width.

howToUse.label: Generate PDF
howToUse.description: Click the Generate PDF button. The file is created entirely in your browser and downloaded automatically.

faq.question: What image formats can I use?
faq.answer: Any image format your browser supports: PNG, JPG, WebP, BMP, GIF, and more.

faq.question: What does "Original" page size mean?
faq.answer: Each page is sized to match the pixel dimensions of the corresponding image at 96 DPI, preserving the image at its natural scale.

faq.question: Can I reorder the images?
faq.answer: Yes. Drag the rows in the image list to rearrange them. The PDF pages will follow the same order.

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.answer: No. The PDF is generated entirely in your browser using the jsPDF library. Your images never leave your device.

faq.question: Is there a limit on the number of images?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but many large images may slow down your browser. Consider resizing very large photos before converting.
